purpose: Close four reviewed exchange candidates after promotion through D-1000 BX23.

entries.notes: BX23 promotes Kumbaya after current first-party application review, MegaETH ecosystem confirmation, and independent TVL, fee, revenue, and DEX-volume verification. Swap, pool, router, launchpad, and social-asset surfaces remain one entity.
BX23 promotes SakuraSwap after current first-party trading and pool review plus independent multichain TVL, fee, and DEX-volume verification. AMM, CLMM, and network deployments remain one entity.

BX23 promotes the current Neoxa-team trading venue as limited. Custody and manual withdrawal-review features support centralized exchange-like modeling, and the venue remains distinct from the separately planned native Neoxa DEX.
BX23 promotes SquidSwap as limited because current directory markets remain non-zero while first-party domain continuity is uncertain. The canonical URL is preserved without a live assertion.
